intended_use: Axial3D Insight is intended for use as a cloud-based service and image segmentation framework for the transfer of DICOM imaging information from a medical scanner to an output file. The Axial3D Insigh
intended_use_full: Axial3D Insight is intended for use as a cloud-based service and image segmentation framework for the transfer of DICOM imaging information from a medical scanner to an output file. The Axial3D Insight output file can be used for the fabrication of physical replicas of the output file using additive manufacturing methods. The output file or physical replica can be used for treatment planning. The output file or physical replica can be used for diagnostic purposes in the field of trauma, orthopedic, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular applications. Axial3D Insight should be used with other diagnostic tools and expert clinical judgment.
target_condition: Trauma, orthopedic, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular conditions requiring surgical or diagnostic planning
ground_truth: For machine learning validation: imaging data from multiple scanner manufacturers (GE, Siemens, Philips, Toshiba). For clinical validation: radiologist and physician review using RADPEER assessment framework.
other_metrics: Clinical Segmentation Performance Study: 3 radiologists reviewed 12 cases across orthopedics, trauma, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular fields; all cases scored within acceptance criteria of 1 or 2a using RADPEER framework. Intended Use Validation Study: 9 physicians reviewed 12 cases across orthopedics, trauma, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular, confirming 3D model outputs satisfied end-user needs. Phantom Testing: Stratasys Origin One and J850 printers verified to achieve ±0.3mm geometric accuracy using NIST test phantoms.
